seoKeywords: how much paint do I need
explanation:
# Paint Coverage Calculator

Accurate material calculations for home improvement and construction projects prevent overspending and wasted trips to the hardware store. This calculator applies proven industry standards for paint coverage.

## How Paint Coverage Works

Paint coverage is typically measured in square meters per liter. Standard interior latex paint covers approximately 10-12 m² per liter with one coat. Professional results usually require two coats, so multiply your single-coat calculation by 2.

Factors that affect coverage include paint quality, wall texture, color intensity, and application technique. This calculator uses industry-standard estimates.

## How to Use This Calculator

1. Enter the total wall area you plan to paint (in m²)
2. Enter the coverage rate from your paint can (typically 10-12 m² per liter)
3. The calculator determines total liters needed for one coat
4. For professional results, plan for 2 coats

## Important Notes

This calculation is an estimate for reference purposes. For critical decisions, consult with a painting professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How much paint do I need for my walls?
faq.a: Enter your wall area in square meters and the paint's coverage rate (found on the can). The calculator shows how many liters you'll need for one coat. Multiply by 2 for typical two-coat coverage.
Paint coverage is measured in m² per liter. Standard interior paint covers 10-12 m² per liter. The formula is simple: Wall Area ÷ Coverage Rate = Liters Needed.

faq.q: Should I always use two coats of paint?
faq.a: Yes, professional results typically require two coats. One coat rarely provides complete coverage, especially when painting over darker colors or different finishes.

faq.q: What's the difference between paint and primer coverage?
faq.a: Primer coverage is slightly higher (10-14 m² per liter). Apply primer first, then calculate your finish paint coverage separately. Both apply two coats for best results.

faq.q: Why does paint coverage vary between brands?
faq.a: Coverage depends on paint quality, formulation, wall texture, application method, and color intensity. Premium paints often have slightly lower coverage rates but provide better durability.
Results are typically within ±5-10% of actual requirements, depending on paint quality, surface condition, and application technique. Always add 10% extra for waste.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes, this calculator and all our tools are completely free with no registration required.

faq.q: Do you save my calculation data?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen directly in your browser. We never send your data to any server or store it anywhere.
